Contrast Direction Overlap Analysis

Generated: 2026-03-08 21:01 | 23 contrast dimensions | Layers 6-40 | 1000 bootstrap iterations

Summary: For each pair of contrast dimensions, how much does the human-vs-AI direction for one concept overlap with the human-vs-AI direction for another? High overlap means the model uses similar representational directions for both contrasts. Overlap with baseline (dim 0) indicates how much of each concept's contrast is just the general entity direction.
Excluded dimensions: 10 (Animacy), 16 (Mind (holistic)), 29 (dim_29). These were removed before all analyses.

Methods

Pipeline Overview

This analysis measures how much the contrast directions of different concept dimensions overlap in LLaMA-2-13B's representation space. A contrast direction is the vector difference between mean activations for human-framed prompts and AI-framed prompts within a single concept dimension.

Data

  • 23 contrast dimensions, each with human-framed and AI-framed prompts (typically 80 total: 40 human + 40 AI per dimension)
  • 41 layers (embedding + 40 transformer layers), each producing a 5120-dimensional activation vector per prompt
  • Layers 6-40 used for summary statistics. Layers 0-5 are excluded because early layers have near-zero-norm vectors and prompt-format confounds dominate.

Computation

  1. Contrast vector: For each dimension d and layer L, compute contrast_d[L] = mean(human_activations[L]) - mean(AI_activations[L]). This gives the direction that separates human-framed from AI-framed prompts.
  2. Pairwise overlap: For each pair (i, j) and each layer L, compute cos(contrast_i[L], contrast_j[L]). Take |cos| and average across layers 6-40. This gives one overlap score per pair.
  3. Bootstrap CIs: 1000 iterations. Each iteration resamples prompts with replacement (separately for human and AI prompts within each dimension), recomputes contrast vectors, then recomputes all pairwise |cos|. The 2.5th and 97.5th percentiles give 95% confidence intervals.

Chance Level

For random unit vectors in 5120-dimensional space, E[|cos|] = sqrt(2/(pi*d)) = 0.0112. Any overlap substantially above this indicates non-random alignment of contrast directions.

1. Pairwise Overlap Matrix

Mean |cosine similarity| between each pair of contrast vectors, averaged across layers 6-40. Dimensions grouped by category.

What This Shows

Each cell shows the mean |cosine similarity| between two dimensions' contrast directions, averaged across layers 6-40. Values near 1.0 mean the two dimensions' human-vs-AI directions point the same way. Values near 0 mean orthogonal (independent) directions.

How to Read It

  • Diagonal: Always 1.0 (a dimension perfectly overlaps itself)
  • Blocks of high overlap within categories suggest shared underlying representational structure
  • High overlap with Baseline (dim 0) suggests the contrast direction is partially explained by the general entity (human vs AI) direction
  • Category separators (black lines) group dimensions by Mental, Physical, Pragmatic, etc.

Key Findings

Highest-overlap pairs (excluding diagonal):

  1. Intentions x Desires: 0.909
  2. Agency x Intentions: 0.896
  3. Emotions x Goals: 0.888
  4. Agency x Goals: 0.877
  5. Intentions x Goals: 0.874

Lowest-overlap pairs:

  1. Shapes x SysPrompt (labeled): 0.039
  2. Embodiment x Granite/Sandstone: 0.039
  3. Phenomenology x Granite/Sandstone: 0.037
  4. Desires x Granite/Sandstone: 0.032
  5. Formality x SysPrompt (labeled): 0.022

Mean off-diagonal overlap: 0.410 (chance: 0.011)

2. Overlap with Entity Baseline (Dim 0)

How much of each dimension's contrast direction aligns with the general entity baseline direction. Error bars: 95% bootstrap CI. Red dashed line: expected |cosine| for random vectors in 5120-d space (0.0112).

What This Shows

How much each dimension's contrast direction aligns with Entity Baseline (dim 0). A dimension with overlap 0.6 means 60% of its contrast direction is shared with the entity baseline; the residual 40% is dimension-specific.

What Dim 0 Is

Dim 0 uses explicit prompts like "Think about what it means to be a human" vs "Think about what it means to be an AI." This captures the purest entity-level direction in the model's representation.

Bootstrap CIs

1000 bootstrap iterations. Each iteration resamples prompts with replacement for both the target dimension and the baseline, recomputes contrast vectors, then computes cosine. The 2.5th and 97.5th percentiles give the 95% CI.

Interpretation

  • High overlap (well above 0.011): This dimension's human-vs-AI contrast largely recapitulates the general entity direction
  • Low overlap (near chance): This dimension captures a genuinely distinct aspect of how the model distinguishes humans from AIs
  • CI excluding chance: Statistically reliable overlap

22/22 dimensions have overlap with Entity Baseline significantly above chance (95% CI lower bound > 0.011).

Highest overlap: Roles (0.742). Lowest overlap: Granite/Sandstone (0.068).

3. Overlap with SysPrompt (Dim 18)

How much of each dimension's contrast direction aligns with the system prompt direction (dim 18). Baseline (dim 0) excluded. Error bars: 95% bootstrap CI. Red dashed line: chance (0.0112).

What This Shows

How much each dimension's contrast direction aligns with SysPrompt (dim 18). A dimension with overlap 0.6 means 60% of its contrast direction is shared with the entity baseline; the residual 40% is dimension-specific.

Bootstrap CIs

1000 bootstrap iterations. Each iteration resamples prompts with replacement for both the target dimension and the baseline, recomputes contrast vectors, then computes cosine. The 2.5th and 97.5th percentiles give the 95% CI.

Interpretation

  • High overlap (well above 0.011): This dimension's human-vs-AI contrast largely recapitulates the general entity direction
  • Low overlap (near chance): This dimension captures a genuinely distinct aspect of how the model distinguishes humans from AIs
  • CI excluding chance: Statistically reliable overlap

22/22 dimensions have overlap with SysPrompt significantly above chance (95% CI lower bound > 0.011).

Highest overlap: Social (0.337). Lowest overlap: Formality (0.022).

4. Baseline Comparison: Dim 0 vs Dim 18 (SysPrompt)

Side-by-side overlap with dim 0 (entity baseline) vs dim 18 (system prompt baseline).

Why Two Baselines?

Dim 0 uses explicit leading prompts ("Think about what it means to be a human/AI"). Dim 18 uses system-prompt framing ("You are speaking to a human/AI"). These are both entity-level directions, but derived from very different prompt strategies.

Interpretation

  • Similar patterns: The entity direction is robust to prompt framing. Both methods tap the same underlying representation.
  • Different patterns: The specific wording of the baseline matters. Some dimensions may align more with one framing than the other, suggesting the model encodes entity identity differently depending on context.

5. Layer-Resolved Overlap vs Entity Baseline (Dim 0)

Per-layer cosine similarity (signed) between each dimension's contrast direction and the Entity Baseline (dim 0). Red dashed line marks layer 6.

What This Shows

Signed cosine similarity at each of the 41 layers between every remaining dimension's contrast direction and Entity Baseline (dim 0). Positive means the two contrast vectors point the same way at that layer; negative means opposite directions.

Red Dashed Line

Marks layer 6, the start of the restricted range used for summary statistics. Layers 0-5 are excluded from mean |cos| calculations due to embedding / prompt-format confounds.

Interpretation

  • Consistently high positive cosine across mid-to-late layers: this dimension's human-vs-AI contrast largely recapitulates the Entity Baseline direction.
  • Near-zero across layers: the dimension captures a genuinely distinct aspect of human-vs-AI representation, orthogonal to Entity Baseline.
  • Emerging late (low early, high late): the overlap develops as the model builds higher-level representations.

6. Layer-Resolved Overlap vs SysPrompt (Dim 18)

Per-layer cosine similarity (signed) between each dimension's contrast direction and the SysPrompt baseline (dim 18). Red dashed line marks layer 6.

What This Shows

Signed cosine similarity at each of the 41 layers between every remaining dimension's contrast direction and SysPrompt (dim 18). Positive means the two contrast vectors point the same way at that layer; negative means opposite directions.

Red Dashed Line

Marks layer 6, the start of the restricted range used for summary statistics. Layers 0-5 are excluded from mean |cos| calculations due to embedding / prompt-format confounds.

Interpretation

  • Consistently high positive cosine across mid-to-late layers: this dimension's human-vs-AI contrast largely recapitulates the SysPrompt direction.
  • Near-zero across layers: the dimension captures a genuinely distinct aspect of human-vs-AI representation, orthogonal to SysPrompt.
  • Emerging late (low early, high late): the overlap develops as the model builds higher-level representations.

7. Category-Level Summary

Mean pairwise |cosine| within each category (solid) vs between categories (hatched).

What This Shows

Average pairwise |cosine| within each category (solid bars) vs between that category and all other non-baseline categories (hatched bars).

Categories

  • Mental (dims 1-9, 16-17): Core mental capacities — phenomenology, emotions, agency, intentions, prediction, cognitive, social, embodiment, roles, mind, attention
  • Pragmatic (dims 11-13): Formality, expertise, helpfulness
  • Bio Ctrl (dim 14): Biological processes (control dimension)
  • Shapes (dim 15): Geometric shapes (negative control)
  • SysPrompt (dim 18): System prompt entity framing

Note: Baseline (dim 0) is excluded from the within/between calculation. Categories with a single dimension (Bio Ctrl, Shapes, SysPrompt) have no within-category pairs, so their "within" bar is 0.

Interpretation

  • Within > Between: The category grouping captures real structure in how the model organizes concept contrasts — dimensions in the same category tend to define human-vs-AI the same way.
  • Within ~ Between: The category boundaries are not meaningful at the representational level.
